exitCriteria: Solve from scratch without references.
Explain the invariant, complexity, and one failure case.
Add one edge-case test before marking it reviewed.
Model state, behavior, and extension points explicitly.
Explain the tradeoff behind each class/interface boundary.
Prove one mutation or interaction path with a tiny test.
State assumptions, bottlenecks, and failure modes.
Choose storage, cache, queue, and deploy boundaries deliberately.
Describe how you would observe and roll back the design in production.
Tell one concrete story with situation, action, result, and lesson.
Name the tradeoff you made and what you would do differently.
Practice one skeptical follow-up answer.
artifacts: Evidence or metric from the story

exitCriteria: Pass shape or parity checks for the concept.
Explain the forward path and the failure mode without notes.
Produce an overfit, eval, or checkpoint proof where relevant.
Write expected dimensions for every vector, matrix, and matmul operation.
Pass tiny NumPy shape drills.
Explain where broadcasting is allowed and where it should fail.
Train a tiny model with a visible loss decrease.
Explain learning rate, gradient step, and why loss can diverge.
Compare at least two optimizer settings.
Trace one forward and backward pass.
Verify gradients against a finite-difference or autograd reference.
Explain the chain rule path in plain English.
Compute one softmax cross-entropy example by hand.
Use a numerically stable implementation.
Connect loss to next-token prediction.
Show moment updates and weight decay separately.
Explain AdamW vs Adam weight decay.
Run one gradient clipping failure drill.
Encode and decode text losslessly.
Document byte-level vs BPE tradeoffs.
Pass round-trip tests for edge cases.
Train a next-token model with train/validation loss.
Generate samples from the trained model.
Explain perplexity and context-window limits.
Compare greedy, temperature, and top-k decoding.
Explain repetition and randomness failure modes.
Save fixed-prompt outputs for comparison.
Trace token and position embedding shapes.
Explain context length and tied weights.
Pass an output projection shape test.
Implement Q/K/V attention with causal masking.
Pass shape and mask tests.
Explain why future tokens are hidden.
Split and rejoin heads correctly.
Pass head-dimension shape assertions.
Explain output projection and parallel heads.
Build pre-LayerNorm, attention, MLP, and residual paths.
Pass full block shape tests.
Overfit a tiny repeated dataset.
Run seeded training with train/validation loss.
Catch NaNs or exploding gradients.
Prove the model can overfit 1-10 KB of text.
Save model, optimizer, config, and dataset manifest.
Reload and resume training.
Verify deterministic behavior after restore.
Inject LoRA into selected linear layers.
Freeze base weights and save adapter-only checkpoints.
Compare base, few-shot, LoRA, and LoRA plus retrieval.
Clean, dedupe, and format JSONL examples.
Add leakage and memorization checks.
Document what style signal the data should teach.
Run work inside a Web Worker.
Persist checkpoint state through reload.
Keep UI responsive during inference or training shell work.
Implement one WGSL compute kernel.
Match CPU/WASM output within tolerance.
Measure whether acceleration is real.
Run held-out prompts against multiple baselines.
Check for leakage, copying, and hallucination.
Write a pass/fail decision before more training.
